sections.paragraph:
πιστῶς adv. of πιστός (since Antiphon Or. 2, 47; ins [SEG XLII, 1215, 6], pap [PLond II, 301, 7f p. 256: II a.d.]; 4 Km 16:2; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 44) pert. to being reliable, faithfully στηρίζεσθαι 1 Cl 35:5; φυλάσσειν Dg 7:2.—M-M s.v. πιστός.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 821
